div.h2: sha256_finalize() Padding — One-Block vs Two-Block Path

div.p: Entry ctx→buf[0..buf_len-1] = unprocessed bytes ctx→buf_len ∈ [0, 63] ctx→total_bits = accumulated bit count

memset(buf + used, 0, 64 − used) Zero-fill buf[used..63] (7 bytes max if buf_len=56) ───────────────────────── sha256_compress(H, buf) Compress block 1 (contains msg tail + 0x80 + zeros) ───────────────────────── memset(buf, 0, 64) Clear entire buf for block 2 ⚠ MUST zero ALL bytes — stale bytes → wrong digest
memset(buf + used, 0, 56 − used) Zero-fill buf[used..55] (0 bytes if buf_len=55 / used=56) ───────────────────────── No compression yet. Length field slots buf[56..63] are in the SAME block.

L = ctx→total_bits buf[56] = (L >> 56) & 0xFF ← MSB buf[57] = (L >> 48) & 0xFF buf[58] = (L >> 40) & 0xFF buf[59] = (L >> 32) & 0xFF buf[60] = (L >> 24) & 0xFF buf[61] = (L >> 16) & 0xFF buf[62] = (L >> 8) & 0xFF buf[63] = (L ) & 0xFF ← LSB ───────────────────────────────── ⚠ Use total_bits, NOT buf_len×8 ⚠ Explicit shifts — NOT memcpy (memcpy byte-reverses on x86)
sha256_compress(ctx→H, ctx→buf) Block contains: msg bytes (if one-block path) 0x80 marker zero fill 8-byte length field ───────────────────── ctx→H[0..7] now holds the final hash state.

for i = 0..7: digest[i×4+0] = (H[i] >> 24) & 0xFF ← MSB digest[i×4+1] = (H[i] >> 16) & 0xFF digest[i×4+2] = (H[i] >> 8) & 0xFF digest[i×4+3] = (H[i] ) & 0xFF ← LSB ──────────────────────────────────────── ⚠ NOT memcpy(digest, H, 32) — little-endian platforms write bytes reversed Output: digest[0..31] = 32-byte SHA-256 hash
digest[0..31] = correct SHA-256 hash ctx state = indeterminate Call sha256_init() before reuse
Boundary Analysis buf_len=55 → used=56 → 56>56? NO → one block buf_len=56 → used=57 → 57>56? YES → two blocks ← THE TRAP buf_len=0 → used=1 → 1>56? NO → one block (empty msg)
One-Block Example: "abc" (buf_len=3) used = 4 buf: [61][62][63][80][00×52][00×7][18] 1 compress call total
